sglang.title: [Bugfix] Fix inverted PP-disable condition for diffusion LLM inference
sglang.author: Ronnie-Rui
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
_handle_dllm_inference() is intended to disable pipeline parallelism for diffusion LLM inference because this path does not support pp_size > 1.
However, the current guard is inverted:
if not self.pp_size > 1:
    ...
    self.pp_size = 1
This means:
when pp_size > 1, the unsupported pipe

sglang.title: [AMD-miles] Add a ROCm 7.0 MI35x miles nightly image
sglang.author: JessicaJiang-123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Co-authored-with: @XinyuJiangCMU

Motivation

Miles need a ROCm 7.0 image for MI35x: ROCm 7.2 has a known VMM physical-memory-release regression that prevents torch_memory_saver from releasing VRAM on colocate sleep/wake, which OOMs colocate RL training on MI350X. A ROCm 7.0 base avoids it. This PR

Ensure the ceiling is at least the global max token budget.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Support non-power-of-2 top_k in legacy triton_kernels routing
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The bundled (v3.5.1) triton_kernels legacy routing path fails to compile for non-power-of-2 top_k. _routing_compute_indx does tl.arange(0, N_EXPTS_ACT * BLOCK_M), and tl.arange requires a power-of-2 length — so e.g. DeepSeek-V4 with top_k=6 (6 * 32 = 192) raises a compile error and the model

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Support -1 (invalid/non-local) slots in topk_ids for Triton MoE
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Under expert parallelism, dispatch hands the experts a topk_ids tensor whose entries can be -1, marking invalid / non-local slots (a token's routed expert lives on another rank). The Triton unfused MoE path (gpt_oss_triton_kernels_moe.py) mishandled these in two places:

expert_map[topk_ids]

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Normalize slashes in Helion GPU names

Purpose

Helion uses canonicalize_gpu_name() to build stable GPU identifiers. ROCm can report composite GPU names such as AMD Instinct MI250X / MI250; the slash currently survives normalization, which makes the identifier unsuitable for config lookup or path-like keys.

Motivation
In MooncakeStoreConnector, each KV-load request pays control overhead before any RDMA transfer begins: Python-side request preparation (token/block walk, slice list construction, tp-rank rotation, disk-offload budgeting) plus the master key lookup that runs inside batch_get_into_multi_buf

vllm.author: xyang16
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR add swap AB optimization to fused_moe_kernel to make better use of WGMMA. Similar as #20396.
For small BLOCK_SIZE_M cases (BLOCK_SIZE_M < 64), change the gemm from a[M, K] @ b[K, N]-> c[M, N] to b[N, K] @ a[K, M] -> c[N, M].
1% to 4% e2e output token throughput improvement.

vllm.title: [Rust Frontend] Introduce unified parser interface & combined parser
vllm.author: BugenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Move tool-parser and reasoning-parser crates into modules of a unified parser crate (parser/src/tool and parser/src/reasoning). Introduce a UnifiedParser interface, which combines ToolParser and ReasoningParser: it takes decoded text and emits Text | Reasoning | Tool events directly. The cha

vllm.title: [AMD][CI] Fix Pipeline + Context Parallelism test group
vllm.author: aarushjain29
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The Pipeline + Context Parallelism (4 GPUs) group fails on ROCm at test_pp_cudagraph[FLASH_ATTN-2-JackFram/llama-160m].
The test hardcodes --attention-backend=FLASH_ATTN, which resolves to the unified CUDA FlashAttentionBackend on every platform. That backend is not runnable on ROCm:

vllm.title: [CPU] Enable chunked prefill and prefix caching for qwen3.5
vllm.author: tianmu-li
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Removes the guard in vllm/platforms/cpu.py that unconditionally disabled enable_prefix_caching and enable_chunked_prefill for linear-attention models on AMX CPU platforms.
Fix CPUModelRunner._zero_block_ids which is not full-attention blocks in hybrid models (e.g. Qwen3.5). A newly allocate

vllm.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Fix chunk alignment when using context parallelism with TRITON_MLA
vllm.author: micah-wil
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There is a bug in mla_attention when using context parallelism on ROCm. max_context_chunk is being aligned properly on CUDA because of the self.aot_schedule path (which is CUDA-only). The chunk misalignment causes zero accuracy in test_context_parallel.py on ROCm with dcp_size=4 using TRITON_MLA.

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Expand basic correctness target suites
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR splits the basic-correctness target-suite change out of #39238. It updates distributed basic-correctness parametrization so ROCm suites can run the intended two-GPU cases instead of being limited to L4/A100. The target suite now defaults from the detected ROCm accelerator, while preserving t

vllm.title: [Hardware][AMD][CI] Move Metrics, Tracing (2 GPUs) & make optional
vllm.author: mawong-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Move the Metrics, Tracing (2 GPUs) test group to MI325 and make optional for investigation purposes, as we're seeing an unusually high rate of CPU-side segfaults on this test group currently.

vllm.title: [Perf] Enable + tune FlashInfer fused allreduce at world_size=16 on SM 10.3 (GB300)
vllm.author: majunze2001
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
FlashInfer fused allreduce+RMSNorm is gated by world size in two places, and
neither currently admits world_size=16:

PassConfig.flashinfer_max_size() returns None unless the world size is in
FI_SUPPORTED_WORLD_SIZES (was [2, 4, 8]), and
it then looks up FI_ALLREDUCE_FUSION_MAX_SIZE_MB[capab

vllm.title: [SimpleCPUOffloadConnector] Fix remaining global→block conversions under PCP/DCP
vllm.author: majunze2001
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
PR #39831 ("[SimpleCPUOffloadConnector] PCP + DCP support", merged as
c88d3d477) added context-parallel support by scaling KV block sizes by
cp_world_size = pcp_world_size * dcp_world_size. Under CP, each physical
block holds block_size per-rank tokens but represents
block_size * cp_world_si

vllm.title: [ROCm][Perf] Tune wvSplitK on gfx1151
vllm.author: mgehre-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Tune wvSplitK skinny GEMM kernel for gfx1151.
Kernel benchmarks
bf16 wvSplitK kernel benchmark on Strix Halo (gfx1151, 20 CUs):

N=1 average: +18.7% bandwidth improvement
N=2 average: +19.8%
N=3 average: +15.2%
N=4 average: +13.0%

fp16 wvSplitK kernel benchmark:

N=1 average: +6.1% bandwidt
